---
title: "Exploratory_Data_Analysis"
output: pdf_document
date: "2026-04-24"
---

## Class Distribution of Chocaolate Consumption

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

df <- read.csv("drug_consumption_numeric.csv")

ggplot(df, aes(x = choc, fill = choc)) +
  geom_bar() +
  geom_text(stat='count', aes(label=..count..), vjust=-0.5) +
  labs(title = "Class Distribution of Chocolate Consumption",
       x = "Class", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()
```

## Personality Score Distributions by Class

```{r}
library(psych)
describe(df[, c("nscore", "escore", "oscore", "ascore", "cscore")])[, c("mean", "sd", "min", "max", "skew")]
```


```{r}
library(tidyr)

df_long <- df %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(nscore, escore, oscore, ascore, cscore),
               names_to = "trait", values_to = "score")

ggplot(df_long, aes(x = choc, y = score, fill = choc)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  facet_wrap(~ trait, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(title = "Personality Scores by Consumption Class") +
  theme_minimal()
```

## Correlation Heatmap of Personality Scores 

```{r}
library(corrplot)
cor_matrix <- cor(df[, c("nscore","escore","oscore","ascore","cscore")])

par(mar = c(2, 2, 4, 2))

corrplot(cor_matrix, method = "color",
         addCoef.col = "black", tl.col = "black",
         title = "Personality Score Correlations",
         mar = c(1, 1, 2, 1))
```


## Categorical Features 

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(dplyr)

df$choc <- factor(df$choc, levels = c("CL0","CL1","CL2","CL3","CL4","CL5","CL6"))

cat_features <- c("gender", "country", "ethnicity", "age", "education")

for (feat in cat_features) {
  p <- df %>%
    mutate(feature = as.factor(.data[[feat]])) %>%
    group_by(feature, choc) %>%
    summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
    group_by(feature) %>%
    mutate(prop = n / sum(n)) %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = feature, y = prop, fill = choc)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "fill") +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
    labs(title = paste("Chocolate Consumption Class by", feat),
         x = feat, y = "Proportion", fill = "Class") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
  
  print(p)
}
```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(gridExtra)

df$choc <- factor(df$choc, levels = c("CL0","CL1","CL2","CL3","CL4","CL5","CL6"))

plot_proportion <- function(data, feat) {
  data %>%
    mutate(feature = as.factor(.data[[feat]])) %>%
    group_by(feature, choc) %>%
    summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
    group_by(feature) %>%
    mutate(prop = n / sum(n)) %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = feature, y = prop, fill = choc)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "fill") +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
    labs(title = paste("Class by", feat), x = feat, y = "Proportion", fill = "Class") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
          legend.position = "none")  # remove individual legends
}

p1 <- plot_proportion(df, "gender")
p2 <- plot_proportion(df, "age")
p3 <- plot_proportion(df, "country")
p4 <- plot_proportion(df, "education")

legend <- cowplot::get_legend(
  plot_proportion(df, "gender") + theme(legend.position = "right")
)

# Arrange in 2x2 with shared legend
cowplot::plot_grid(
  cowplot::plot_grid(p1, p2, p3, p4, ncol = 2),
  legend,
  rel_widths = c(1, 0.15)
)
```
